title: Action log types, event stream storage, and Level 1 summary computation
purpose: Establish the action log as a query layer over the existing EventStore. Define the ActionLogEntry and Outcome types, implement Level 1 summary computation at tool-call completion, and store structured metadata on existing events. The action log retains full data regardless of context compaction — this is the key value proposition. No new storage backend; everything builds on EventStore and PersistenceSink.
task: Create crates/norn/src/session/action_log.rs with ActionLogEntry (Level 1 summary fields), Outcome enum, and an ActionLog struct that indexes tool-call events from the EventStore. The ActionLog maintains an in-memory index of ActionLogEntry values keyed by tool_call_id. Level 1 summaries are computed at tool-call completion and stored. Level 2 is the existing ToolResult event data (no duplication). Level 3 is populated lazily on first query (non-mutation tools) or eagerly via register_follow_ups (mutation tools — the FollowUpAction already carries before-content). Wire ActionLog into the tool dispatch path to capture entries as tools complete.

requirements.title: Define ActionLogEntry struct with Level 1 fields
requirements.spec: ActionLogEntry SHALL be defined in crates/norn/src/session/action_log.rs with fields: tool_name (String), tool_call_id (String), tool_use_description (String — the model-provided description from the tool use envelope), timestamp (DateTime<Utc>), outcome (Outcome), summary_line (String — one-line result summary like "edit committed: src/handler.rs +5/-3"). ActionLogEntry SHALL derive Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize.
acceptance: crates/norn/src/session/action_log.rs exists
ActionLogEntry has all 6 Level 1 fields
ActionLogEntry derives Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize
serde round-trip test passes

requirements.title: Define Outcome enum
requirements.spec: Outcome SHALL be defined in crates/norn/src/session/action_log.rs with variants: Success, Error { message: String }, Blocked { reason: String }. Outcome SHALL derive Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize.
acceptance: Outcome has exactly 3 variants: Success, Error, Blocked
Error carries a message String
Blocked carries a reason String
serde round-trip test passes for all 3 variants

requirements.title: Define ActionLog struct with in-memory index
requirements.spec: ActionLog SHALL be defined in crates/norn/src/session/action_log.rs. It SHALL hold: entries (IndexMap<String, ActionLogEntry> keyed by tool_call_id preserving insertion order), follow_ups (HashMap<String, Vec<FollowUpAction>> keyed by tool_call_id), original_args (HashMap<String, serde_json::Value> keyed by tool_call_id — the input arguments for each tool call), and a reference to the EventStore for Level 2/3 queries. ActionLog SHALL be thread-safe (parking_lot::RwLock wrapping the inner data).
acceptance: ActionLog struct exists with entries, follow_ups, original_args, and EventStore reference
entries preserves insertion order (IndexMap)
Thread-safe via parking_lot::RwLock
ActionLog::new(event_store: Arc<EventStore>) constructor exists

requirements.title: Level 1 summary computation at tool-call completion
requirements.spec: ActionLog SHALL expose a record_completion method called after each tool dispatch: record_completion(tool_name, tool_call_id, tool_use_description, outcome, output, args, follow_ups). This method SHALL: (1) compute a one-line summary_line from the output (e.g., for edit: "edit committed: {path} +{added}/-{removed}", for error: "error: {first_line}"), (2) create an ActionLogEntry, (3) store it in the entries index, (4) store follow_ups if non-empty, (5) store original args. Summary generation SHALL be a match on tool_name with tool-specific formatters and a generic fallback.
acceptance: record_completion creates an ActionLogEntry and stores it keyed by tool_call_id
Summary line for edit shows path and diff stats
Summary line for read shows path and line count
Summary line for bash shows command (truncated to 80 chars) and exit code
Generic fallback shows tool_name and outcome
Follow-ups stored in follow_ups index
Original args stored in original_args index

requirements.title: Level 2 detail retrieval from EventStore
requirements.spec: ActionLog SHALL expose a get_detail(tool_call_id) method that returns Level 2 data: the ActionLogEntry (Level 1) plus the full tool output JSON, input arguments, duration, and follow-up actions. The tool output is retrieved from the EventStore by finding the ToolResult event matching the tool_call_id. Input arguments come from the original_args index. Follow-ups come from the follow_ups index.
acceptance: get_detail returns Level 1 entry plus full output JSON, args, duration, follow-ups
Output JSON comes from EventStore ToolResult event matching tool_call_id
Returns None for unknown tool_call_id
Level 2 data is available even after context compaction

requirements.title: Level 3 context retrieval with lazy population
requirements.spec: ActionLog SHALL expose a get_context(tool_call_id) method that returns Level 3 data: Level 2 plus raw tool output before compaction, pre/post validate outcomes, and before/after file content. For mutation tools, before-content is sourced from the follow-up action's BeforeContentSource::StoredContent (captured eagerly at register_follow_ups time). For non-mutation tools, Level 3 data is the same as Level 2 (no file content to capture).
acceptance: get_context returns Level 3 data for mutation tools including before-content from follow-up's StoredContent
get_context for non-mutation tools returns same as get_detail (no file snapshots)
Before-content is not stored separately — sourced from FollowUpAction.before_content field
Returns None for unknown tool_call_id

requirements.title: Wire ActionLog into tool dispatch path
requirements.spec: The tool dispatch path (crates/norn/src/loop/tool_dispatch.rs) SHALL call action_log.record_completion after each tool dispatch completes. The ActionLog SHALL be accessible via LoopContext or ToolContext. The ToolResult event in EventStore is already written by the existing dispatch path — the ActionLog adds its own metadata index on top.
acceptance: ActionLog is accessible from LoopContext
record_completion called after every tool dispatch (success and error)
Pre-validate blocks recorded with Outcome::Blocked
Execute errors recorded with Outcome::Error
Successful dispatch recorded with Outcome::Success
Action log queries about its own calls recorded at Level 1 only (CO4 constraint)

requirements.title: Token-efficient Level 1 serialization
requirements.spec: ActionLogEntry SHALL serialize to a compact JSON format suitable for embedding in tool results. A helper method compact_json(&self) -> serde_json::Value SHALL return a minimal representation: {tool, id, desc, ts, outcome, summary}. Short field names keep token count low. Scanning 100 entries via compact_json SHALL produce under 1000 tokens.
acceptance: compact_json uses short field names: tool, id, desc, ts, outcome, summary
100 entries with typical values (tool=edit, desc=20 chars, summary=40 chars) produce under 1000 tokens estimated at 4 chars/token
compact_json output is valid JSON parseable by serde_json

requirements.title: Unit tests for ActionLog
requirements.spec: Tests SHALL verify: (1) record_completion creates entries retrievable by tool_call_id, (2) entries preserve insertion order, (3) get_detail returns Level 2 data, (4) get_context returns Level 3 data with before-content from follow-up, (5) Outcome serde round-trips, (6) compact_json has correct field names and is compact, (7) self-referential query entries logged at Level 1 only.
acceptance: Test: record_completion + list entries returns in order
Test: get_detail returns output and args for known tool_call_id
Test: get_detail returns None for unknown tool_call_id
Test: compact_json output has exactly 6 keys
Test: Outcome serde round-trip for all 3 variants
All tests pass with cargo test -p norn

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the action_log query tool — that is NTA-002
SHALL NOT implement the mutation ledger — that is NTA-003
SHALL NOT implement follow-up discovery queries — that is NTA-004
SHALL NOT implement the follow_up tool — that is NTF-003
SHALL NOT implement register_follow_ups on any tools — that is NTF-002/NTF-004
SHALL NOT introduce a new storage backend or file format — EventStore only
verification: cargo check -p norn succeeds with zero errors
cargo clippy -p norn -- -D warnings produces zero errors
cargo fmt -p norn -- --check produces no diff
cargo test -p norn passes all tests
ActionLog is accessible from LoopContext and records every tool dispatch
